game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles III: The Manhattan Project

danger_signals.meaning: Active Shredder/Foot Clan threat in current area

danger_signals.visual_form: Large, distinct enemy sprites different from standard troops
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard or strengthened Foot Clan resistance

danger_signals.visual_form: Unique sprite design, significantly larger than standard enemies (Shredder, Krang, Bebop, Rocksteady, etc.)
danger_signals.meaning: Stage culmination threat; major narrative/gameplay checkpoint

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental backdrop showing island suspended above water/sky
danger_signals.meaning: Shredder's threat is active and environmental; world-state has been altered by antagonist

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct futuristic mechanical environment distinct from organic/urban stages
danger_signals.meaning: Progression into Krang's domain; escalating danger level

safety_signals.visual_form: Menu interface showing four turtles (Leonardo, Raphael, Michelangelo, Donatello) with signature weapons
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone/preparation state before gameplay engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Tropical, open environment with vacation atmosphere
safety_signals.meaning: Story introduction zone; lower initial threat before Manhattan crisis escalates
Resource availability; player agency in resource management

safety_signals.visual_form: Menu option for friendly-fire toggle (Famicom: 'game type A'; NES: separate mode)
safety_signals.meaning: Player control over difficulty and cooperation parameters; safety net for groups

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Foot Clan uniform (visible on Foot Soldiers enemy sprites)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard Foot Soldiers, Giant Mousers, Stone Warriors, mutant lieutenants (Bebop, Rocksteady, Tokka, Rahzar)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Technodrome structure; Shredder's lair; floating Manhattan island

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Krang boss form; Super Shredder (mutated film version); Krang's Spaceship enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Krang's Spaceship stage (final level); Technodrome as shared Foot Clan/Krang facility

faction_indicators.color_identity: Individual turtle colors (Leo: Blue, Raph: Red, Mikey: Orange, Donnie: Purple)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Signature weapons (katanas, sai, nunchaku, bo staff); Turtle silhouette
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player sprite available in four distinct variations with weapon differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Story progression from Florida to Manhattan to Technodrome; April O'Neil rescue objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Beach environment, tropical setting, April O'Neil broadcasting normally
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Turtles in leisure state; low enemy density; introductory difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shredder cutscene (sprite overlay with mask covering full face render); April held hostage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story trigger; enemy forces escalate; stage transitions begin

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental backdrop showing Manhattan suspended; sky/water backdrop; architectural destruction implications
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Foot Clan occupation of urban environments; boss encounters intensify; stage design reflects technological alteration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mechanical/futuristic environment distinct from urban/organic stages; distinct color palette and tile design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Krang presence; advanced enemy types; mid-game difficulty spike

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final confrontation with Super Shredder and Krang
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Krang's Spaceship environment; Super Shredder boss sprite (film-based mutant variant)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss battle; maximum difficulty; narrative climax visual staging

environmental_storytelling.example: Journey from Florida beach (peaceful vacation) → Manhattan streets (urban crisis) → Technodrome (antagonist infrastructure) → Krang's Spaceship (climactic confrontation). Visual environment shift communicates narrative escalation without dialogue.

Standard Foot Soldiers indicate basic Foot Clan presence; appearance of Giant Mousers/Stone Warriors signals environmental hazards or Shredder's technological advantage; boss encounters mark stage narrative beats.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character sprite design as faction communication
environmental_storytelling.example: Four distinct player turtles with color-coded weapons and unique special attacks; enemy Foot Clan uniforms distinguish antagonist affiliation; Super Shredder visual design (film-specific mutation) communicates threat escalation.

Shredder's hijacked broadcast appearance (mask sprite overlay on revealed face) communicates both his presence and narrative agency; April's hostage state visually establishes motivation for turtle progression.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Static, speaking/threatening pose; visually dominant; April held as visible hostage
Standard melee approach; uniform movement patterns; susceptible to player special attacks
Larger, more dangerous patrol pattern; indicates environmental amplification of threat
Distinct locomotion per character; signature attack animations tied to weapon identity

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Boss characters (Dirtbag, Groundchuck, Slash, Leatherhead, Bebop, Rocksteady, Tokka, Rahzar, Super Shredder)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Unique attack patterns; larger sprite scale; health bar visibility; thematic environmental design per boss
Stationary in Shredder's presence; rescue triggers stage progression

notes: TMNT III is an original NES title designed for beat-em-up genre conventions. Visual communication relies heavily on sprite-based distinction (color, size, uniqueness) to convey faction, threat level, and narrative progression. The game uses environmental backdrops (beach → urban Manhattan → Technodrome → spaceship) as primary world-state indicators. Anti-piracy measures (damage reduction, invincibility toggles, cheat disabling) are behavioral mechanics tied to ROM integrity, not player-facing world-state manifestation. Regional differences (US vs. Famicom) are primarily menu-based, not visual-manifestation differences in active gameplay. The two-player mode toggling (friendly fire on/off) creates two distinct behavioral world-states with identical visuals.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (TMNT III debug modes, regional differences, anti-piracy, hidden graphics)
